title.zh: 沉在南太平洋、等待星位的非欧几里得石城
title.en: The Drowned City Waiting Beneath Impossible Angles

summary.zh: 克苏鲁的沉眠之城，建筑角度违背人类空间直觉；当地震与群星暂时相合，岛屿便从海下浮出。
summary.en: Cthulhu’s drowned city, built in angles hostile to human perception and raised briefly when earthquake meets the appointed stars.

description.zh:
拉莱耶沉在南太平洋深处，是《克苏鲁的呼唤》三组文献最终汇合的地点。地震令一片黏湿的黑色石岛短暂露出，水手登岸后发现门、墙和地面的关系无法由熟悉的几何判断：看似锐角的地方会把人吞下，看似水平的表面忽然成为陡坡。城市的异常远超“长得古怪”：它让观察者对上下、内外与距离的信任逐层破裂。

克苏鲁被封在巨门之后，等待群星允许城市与世界重新接通。小说给出近似坐标，却把它放在地图上也不能驯服的区域；物理位置与宇宙时机必须同时吻合，拉莱耶才真正存在于人类可达的现实。后世地图、游戏与影视不断扩建这座沉城，原作却只让探险者窥见一小段登陆路线。拉莱耶因此更像一枚偶尔浮出海面的句号：它证明所有前置档案都指向某处，也证明抵达不等于理解。
description.en:
R’lyeh lies beneath the South Pacific, the point at which the three documentary movements of “The Call of Cthulhu” converge. An earthquake briefly raises an island of wet black stone. Sailors landing there find that doors, walls, and ground cannot be judged by familiar geometry: an apparently acute angle swallows a man, while a level surface becomes a precipice. The city is not merely strange in appearance. It dismantles the observer’s trust in up and down, interior and exterior, nearness and distance.

Cthulhu is sealed beyond a tremendous portal, waiting until the stars permit the city to reconnect with the world. The story offers an approximate coordinate but places R’lyeh in a region no map can domesticate. Physical location and cosmic timing must coincide before it fully enters accessible reality. Later games, maps, and films have built vast districts around the drowned metropolis; Lovecraft allows his sailors only a fragment of an approach. R’lyeh is a period that surfaces briefly from the sea, proving both that every preceding document pointed somewhere and that arrival does not produce understanding.

sources.zh: H. P. 洛夫克拉夫特《克苏鲁的呼唤》
sources.en: H. P. Lovecraft, “The Call of Cthulhu”

sources.zh: S. T. 乔希、David E. Schultz《H. P. Lovecraft Encyclopedia》
sources.en: S. T. Joshi and David E. Schultz, An H. P. Lovecraft Encyclopedia

label.zh: 小说中的拉莱耶近似坐标
